TheoreticalBasisBubble: JD-R resource logic: manager interpersonal skill functions as a job resource enabling motivation and wellbeing.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Multi-study scale development and validation; report omega (not alpha only); aggregate only if ICC and agreement support manager-level inference; use shrinkage for small n managers.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Source set is general org/IO psych with validation samples described in Beenen et al. (open access). Exact sector mix varies by study; treat as cross-sector evidence.
TechApplicabilityBubble: High relevance to knowledge work where interpersonal coordination and discretionary effort matter.
CalculationBubble: 21 items, 5-point anchors 1 not at all true of my supervisor to 5 very true; score facet means and total mean; aggregate to manager mean with minimum rater rules and ICC checks; empirical Bayes shrinkage recommended.
KnownBiasesBubble: Halo and liking effects; mood and recent events; selection into teams/projects; common-source inflation if outcomes collected same wave.
SourcesBubble: Beenen et al. (2021) Frontiers in Psychology, doi:10.3389/fpsyg.2021.631390.
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

TheoreticalBasisBubble: Developmental leadership as a resource enabling learning and performance improvement.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Primary scale development plus emerging longitudinal validation; recommend pairing with feedback environment to separate personal skill from system.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Evidence includes field settings; your instrument choice is positioned as usable in tech and knowledge work (development and learning cycles).
TechApplicabilityBubble: Strong fit for tech where learning loops and role clarity matter.
CalculationBubble: 8 items; common implementation uses 7-point frequency anchors 1 almost never to 7 almost always; score mean; aggregate to manager mean; report omega.
KnownBiasesBubble: Confounded with formal review cadence; coaching salience spikes after performance problems; avoid equating coaching with meeting frequency.
SourcesBubble: Ellinger et al. (2003) doi:10.1002/hrdq.1078; Stone et al. (2024) doi:10.3389/fpsyg.2024.1277422.
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

MetricName: Leader Autonomy Support (LAS) via Work Climate Questionnaire (WCQ)
TheoreticalBasisBubble: Self-Determination Theory: autonomy support promotes need satisfaction and healthier motivation, especially relevant in cognitively demanding work.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Meta-analytic evidence; treat job design autonomy as a confound; standardize within job family/level.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Evidence base is broad across work settings; tech relevance is explicitly called out because autonomy is salient in knowledge work.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Strong applicability in tech; interpret jointly with role constraints and hybrid policy.
CalculationBubble: WCQ long form 15 items; 7-point anchors 1 not at all true to 7 very true; score mean; aggregate to manager with minimum rater rules and ICC. Licensing: academic use permitted with terms; commercial use requires permission via SDT center.
KnownBiasesBubble: Confounded with job design autonomy; hybrid policies alter perceived autonomy; response-style differences across groups.
SourcesBubble: Baard et al. (2004) doi:10.1111/j.1559-1816.2004.tb02690.x; Slemp et al. (2018) meta-analytic review (as cited in your MEI table).
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

TheoreticalBasisBubble: Empowerment and SDT-aligned leadership (autonomy support and development support dimensions).
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Scale development with validated factor structure; use documented scoring; include as “empowerment” dimension in LBI.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Evidence base is general leadership research; your table positions it as appropriate for tech and knowledge work.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Applicable where empowerment is needed for complex problem solving.
CalculationBubble: 18 items; 7-point Likert 1 strongly disagree to 7 strongly agree; score per subscale and overall as documented; retrieve full items from Amundsen and Martinsen (2014) table via library access.
KnownBiasesBubble: Risk of common-method inflation when collected with attitudinal outcomes same wave; confounding with org constraints and role design (interpret with context). (General warning consistent with your framework’s separation of LBI and LOI).
SourcesBubble: Amundsen and Martinsen (2014) The Leadership Quarterly, doi:10.1016/j.leaqua.2013.11.009.
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

PrimaryLevel: Manager (aggregated from direct reports with strict anonymity)
GroundTruthData: Direct-report survey plus HR cases only as supplementary
TheoreticalBasisBubble: Destructive leadership risk; included to prevent harm and as a disqualifying or reverse-scored safeguard.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Meta-analysis with strong links to outcomes; treat as protected signal with escalation protocol, not a normal coaching metric.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Evidence base cross-sector; measurement is sensitive to retaliation dynamics.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Applicable in tech as a safety and ethics guardrail; requires governance pack and privacy thresholds.
CalculationBubble: 15 items; common anchors 1 I cannot remember ever to 5 very often; score mean (higher worse); for MEI reverse-score or treat as threshold; stricter minimum rater and anonymity constraints.
KnownBiasesBubble: Underreporting due to fear; attribution and mood bias; cultural response styles; severe privacy sensitivity.
SourcesBubble: Tepper (2000) Academy of Management Journal; Mackey et al. (2017) meta-analysis doi:10.1177/0149206315573997.
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

MetricName: Leader Member Exchange (LMX-7) plus differentiation diagnostic
TheoreticalBasisBubble: Social exchange and relationship-based leadership; high-quality exchanges predict performance and attitudes.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Meta-analytic and extensive primary evidence; compute mean and optionally dispersion (LMX differentiation) as equity signal; aggregation requires ICC and agreement.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Widely validated across sectors; item anchors vary across studies, so standardize and document your chosen format.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Highly relevant in tech teams where coordination and discretionary effort matter.
CalculationBubble: 7 items; official wording via Graen and Uhl-Bien source; example peer-reviewed anchors: 1 rarely to 5 very often; score mean per rater then manager/team mean; compute within-team SD for differentiation.
KnownBiasesBubble: Anchors vary across publications; differentiation can reflect role allocation or bias; aggregation can hide subgroup differences.
SourcesBubble: Graen and Uhl-Bien (1995) LMX-7 source; operationalisation notes in Beenen et al. (2021).
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

MetricName: Feedback environment (Supervisor Feedback Environment Scale, often short form)
TheoreticalBasisBubble: Feedback environment as a day-to-day developmental context; linked to performance and burnout.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Peer-reviewed scale validation plus meta-analysis; avoid using raw frequency counts of feedback due to gaming risk.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Originally validated broadly; short forms vary (your instrument map flags that short 21-item supervisor form differs from the original 32-item scale).
TechApplicabilityBubble: Strong intervention lever in tech because feedback cycles are central to learning work.
CalculationBubble: Short supervisor form often 21 items; 5-point Likert 1 strongly disagree to 5 strongly agree; score mean overall and or facet means; aggregate to manager mean with ICC and agreement checks; retrieve exact items via Steelman (2004) and Rosen short-form documentation.
KnownBiasesBubble: Confounded with formal performance management cycles; function differences in feedback norms; cross-cultural response styles; gaming if reduced to counts.
SourcesBubble: Steelman et al. (2004) doi:10.1177/0013164403258440; Katz et al. (2021) meta-analysis doi:10.1111/ijsa.12350.
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

TheoreticalBasisBubble: Team learning and voice climate; safe interpersonal risk taking.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Meta-analytic nomological network; compute mean and consider within-team variability as inclusion diagnostic; avoid using as pure rank without invariance checks.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Used widely across industries; sensitive to incident history and justice context.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Very relevant to tech where learning and coordination drive output quality.
CalculationBubble: 7 items; commonly 7-point very inaccurate to very accurate with reverse-scored items; score team mean; aggregation requires ICC and agreement; retrieve item wording via Edmondson (1999) or replication papers that reproduce the full set.
KnownBiasesBubble: Sensitive to recent failures, layoffs, identity dynamics; mean can mask subgroup differences.
SourcesBubble: Edmondson (1999) doi:10.2307/2666999; Frazier et al. (2017) meta-analysis doi:10.1111/peps.12183.
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

TheoreticalBasisBubble: JD-R health impairment pathway; burnout is downstream of demands and low resources.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Strong theoretical and measurement base; treat as LOI and early warning, not a direct manager behaviour score.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Broad evidence base; high privacy sensitivity.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Applicable in tech due to workload cycles, incident response, staffing shocks; interpret with demand controls where ethically governed.
CalculationBubble: MBI-GS often 16 items, MBI-HSS often 22; scoring per manual; if licensing constraints, use peer-reviewed alternative but treat comparability cautiously; report aggregated only.
KnownBiasesBubble: Stigma and response bias; confounded with demand spikes, incidents, staffing; strong privacy sensitivity.
SourcesBubble: Maslach et al. MBI manual (licensed); Demerouti et al. (2001) doi:10.1037/0021-9010.86.3.499; Lesener et al. (2019) JD-R longitudinal meta-analysis doi:10.1080/02678373.2018.1529065.
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

TheoreticalBasisBubble: Treated as demand proxy, not core effectiveness construct; aligns with monitoring and workload fragmentation concerns.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Evidence caution: monitoring has small negative links with job attitudes and small positive links with stress; requires local validation against burnout/engagement before any indexing.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Measurement depends on internal tech stack; must be consent-based, privacy-minimised.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Relevant in tech due to meeting load and distributed teams, but do not include in core MEI until validated internally.
CalculationBubble: Compute metadata-only aggregates (no content): after-hours proportion, meeting hours per week, fragmentation indices; standardize within role/level; enforce k-anonymity thresholds; consider differential privacy for dashboards.
KnownBiasesBubble: Reactance and privacy invasion perceptions; time-zone confounds; role differences; monitoring stigma.
SourcesBubble: Siegel et al. (2022) doi:10.1016/j.chbr.2022.100227; privacy controls: Sweeney (2002) k-anonymity; Dwork et al. (2006) differential privacy.
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

TheoreticalBasisBubble: Same as above; used to test workload fragmentation hypotheses, not as manager effectiveness score without validation.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Evidence caution and governance requirements identical to after-hours metric.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Depends on internal systems; privacy and consent required.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Applicable in tech; interpret alongside incident cycles and role requirements.
CalculationBubble: Meeting hours per FTE per week; calendar fragmentation index; standardize within role/level; validate against burnout and autonomy support before using in any index.
KnownBiasesBubble: Same risks as other monitoring-derived metrics; do not operationalize punitively.
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

TheoreticalBasisBubble: Engagement is a motivational state linked to resources and burnout as distinct constructs.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Foundational scale development and strong psychometrics; treat as LOI and validation target, not sole effectiveness proxy.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Cross-national evidence base; seasonal cycles matter in tech.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Useful for org-level trend and validation of LBI impacts.
CalculationBubble: UWES uses 7-point frequency scale 0 never to 6 always; UWES-9 derived from UWES-17; score subscales and total as mean; justify aggregation with ICC and agreement; test measurement invariance for group comparisons.
KnownBiasesBubble: Seasonal product cycles; transient morale; common-method risk when collected with predictors same wave.
SourcesBubble: Schaufeli et al. (2006) doi:10.1177/0013164405282471; Christian et al. (2011) quantitative review (as cited in your reports).
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

TheoreticalBasisBubble: Withdrawal outcomes are downstream and heavily confounded; use for validation and executive context with risk adjustment.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Strong turnover theory base; needs standardisation within strata and careful causal designs for attribution.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Sector depends on company; internal definition must be auditable.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Especially confounded in tech by reorganisations, labor market shocks, internal mobility policy.
CalculationBubble: Compute quarterly or annualized voluntary turnover rate per manager team; split regrettable vs non-regrettable with documented definition; standardize within job family, level, geography, hybrid status; use as LOI not LBI.
KnownBiasesBubble: Opportunity and project assignment bias; hiring and pay compression effects; selection into teams; Goodhart effects if incentivized.
SourcesBubble: Hom et al. (2017) 100 years of turnover; collective turnover review (as cited in your MEI report).
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar

GroundTruthData: Performance systems, product analytics, sales ops, eng metrics
TheoreticalBasisBubble: Work performance modelling and criterion theory; supervisors matter but outcomes are role-specific.
EvidenceAndValidationBubble: Strong foundation but must be job-specific; treat as LOI and validation criterion; avoid false comparability across roles.
SectorAndSampleBubble: Sector is your company; measurement must be role-specific and audited.
TechApplicabilityBubble: Tech applicability is high if you use role-specific stable indicators and standardize within job family and level.
CalculationBubble: Define job-family performance model (quality, timeliness, throughput or calibrated ratings); standardize within job family/level; control for opportunity, project assignment, team composition; use hierarchical models for manager effects.
KnownBiasesBubble: Goodhart effects; metric drift; opportunity and assignment bias; rating inflation.
SourcesBubble: Campbell and Wiernik (2015) doi:10.1146/annurev-orgpsych-032414-111427; Lazear et al. boss effects (as cited in your report).
CitationCountStatus: Not provided in sources; look up via Scholar
